title: The mess
blocks.0.text: Six reasons crammed into one line. Technically correct — it is a sentence and it sits in a paragraph. But nobody will read it like that.
blocks.2.text: You want each reason on its own line, one below the other. Try. Really try, before you read on.
blocks.3.text:
Press Enter between them — no good, they glue together (you know that already). Put each in its own

— that works, but look at the result: six paragraphs sprawled with gaps between them. It looks like six unrelated thoughts, not a list.
blocks.4.text: Try dashes in front too. Does it work? On screen — sort of. But you have not made a list. You have drawn something that looks like a list.
blocks.6.text: The browser does not know this is a list. Google does not know. The program reading the page aloud will say "dash, the music, dash, the story" — like an idiot.
blocks.7.text: You do not have the tool. The next lesson gives it to you.
blocks.8.text: Remember this feeling — knowing what you want and having nothing to do it with. That is how every developer feels before learning the next thing.
